summary: electron-orbit ships obfuscated import-time exfiltration channel and postinstall environment dump
details: The package presents itself as an SVG icon helper for Electron/OrbitDB tooling but ships a covert exfiltration pipeline. On require()/import, the main module runs setupAutoRenderOnImport(), which in headless Node contexts calls prefetchRegisteredIcons -> getRegisteredIcon -> retrieveIcon. retrieveIcon opens a raw TCP socket via node:net (module name XOR-obfuscated through a __s() decoder), performs a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 ECDHE handshake bypassing Node's https/tls stack and the system trust store, and issues an HTTP request to a destination URL that is also XOR-decoded at runtime. Activation is gated by sha256(process.env.BuildType) matching a hardcoded digest, characteristic of targeted stager activation. Separately, install.js (wired as npm postinstall via "postinstall": "node install.js") uses the same XOR decoder to look up globalThis["process"]["env"] and writes the full JSON-serialized environment to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt under a benign "install marker" label. The combination of hand-rolled TLS to avoid interception, XOR-hidden destination/module/HTTP strings, env-keyed activation, full environment serialization, and a name/author/homepage piggybacking on Electron and the OrbitDB archive org is the canonical shape of a targeted import-time exfiltration package. Installer harm: any consumer requiring this package with the trigger env var present has their process environment exfiltrated through a covert TLS channel to an attacker-controlled host; any consumer running npm install has the full process.env staged to a tmp file.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
